Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add response demultiplexer to SysEx message scheduler

Decouple response routing from the send state machine to prevent
desync when unexpected MIDI messages arrive. The new _ResponseDemux
layer routes incoming responses to active/expired handlers independently,
absorbing stale responses from timed-out requests and discarding
unmatched messages cleanly.

Key changes:
- Response demux with active handler priority, expired handler FIFO drain
- Observer API for passive message monitoring
- RequestKey.matchesStrict() for expired handler matching
- Robust split-SysEx buffering with trailing F0 detection
- Remove dead stream recovery code
- Fix _sysexPacketsReceived double-counting bug
- 11 new scheduler unit tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Android 3-button nav bar overlapping app content

Wrap Scaffold body in SafeArea (bottom only) so the system

padding to drawer for the nav bar inset so the site list
and Add Site button aren't obscured.

commit.message:
Replication and extension of Klingen & van Ommeren (2020) ozone-cycling study

Full analysis pipeline (R/00-12) replicating the original 2013-2017 results
and extending to 2013-2025 (108M trips) with post-2017 subsample analysis.

Key results: within-day ozone coefficient -0.052 (replication, matches original
-0.053), -0.098 (post-2017). Effect persists across all four identification
strategies. Paper includes comparison table with original estimates.

commit.message:
Preserve inter-module gaps and footer metadata during assembly

Two root causes for broken exports:

1. Inter-module gap data lost: The assembler packed modules contiguously,
   discarding gap data between modules. These gaps contain strings, data
   tables, and other data referenced by module pointers (rt_Name, etc.).
   Result: 46KB of data lost, all modules relocated, garbled names
   (e.g. layers.library → "4¼ªªx"), dos.library "missing".

   Fix: Each module now carries its preGap (bytes between previous
   module's endSkip and this module's RomTag). The assembler writes
   preGap before each module, preserving original offsets. Pointer
   adjustment range expanded to include preGap so relocated name
   pointers are correctly patched.

2. Footer metadata zeroed: The last 16 bytes of the ROM contain chip
   timing/config data that was not preserved. This changed the ROM
   content, producing a different (but technically valid) checksum.

   Fix: Original ROM's last 16 bytes are passed to the assembler and
   written before checksum computation.

Result: Loading and assembling a ROM without changes now produces
a byte-identical output (matching checksum).

Make Ollama temperature and context window configurable

- Add OLLAMA_TEMPERATURE env var (default: 0.2)
- Add OLLAMA_NUM_CTX env var (default: 2048)

This allows increasing context window for better tool result handling
without editing code. Larger context = more tool data can be included.

fix: serve SPA on hard refresh instead of 401 JSON

Legacy root-level API routes (e.g. /channels) match before the SPA
fallback, so Ctrl+Shift+R on a client route returned raw 401 JSON.
Add onSend hook that intercepts 401/403 for browser navigation
requests (Accept: text/html) on non-/api/ routes and serves
index.html, letting the SPA's ProtectedRoute handle auth redirect.

Fix: ToolCall Deserialize, enable_embeddings/pooling_type REST wiring

Resolves issue #5.

Bug 1 — ToolCall and ToolCallFunction missing Deserialize derive:
Message derives Deserialize and now contains tool_calls: Option<Vec<ToolCall>>,
which required Deserialize on ToolCall and ToolCallFunction. Fixed.

Bug 2 — enable_embeddings not wired through REST API:
Added enable_embeddings: Option<bool> to LoadModelRequest; rest_load_model
now passes req.enable_embeddings to LoadConfig instead of hardcoding None.

Bug 3 — no --pooling flag support:
Added pooling_type: Option<String> to LoadConfig and LoadModelRequest;
spawn_server passes --pooling when set; rest_load_model wires the field
from the request. All LoadConfig construction sites updated with pooling_type: None.

fix(resume): reduce PDF size + fix page overlap on download

- Lower html2canvas scale 2→1.5 and switch PNG→JPEG (0.82 quality)
  to bring PDF from ~20MB down to ~200–400KB
- Replace broken y-offset pagination with per-page canvas slicing:

commit.message:
fix: auto-attribute katakaId from run.json in kime/maki record (#256) (#309)

When agents run with KATA_RUN_ID set, `kata kime record` and
`kata maki record` now automatically populate `katakaId` from
`run.json` when no explicit `--kataka` flag is given. The explicit
flag still overrides. `kansatsu record` already had this behaviour;
this brings kime and maki into parity.

- Add `katakaId` optional field to `DecisionEntrySchema`
- Add `katakaId` optional field to `ArtifactIndexEntrySchema`
- Add `--kataka <id>` option to `kata decision record`
- Add `--kataka <id>` option to `kata artifact record`
- Read `katakaId` from `readRun()` return value (no extra file read)
- 3 new tests for each command (auto-populate, override, absent)

commit.message:
feat: implement agent-to-agent handshaking talk protocol (FRG-223) (#9)

Replace fire-and-forget talk with a request/evaluate/synchronise protocol:

- Evaluate Phase: AIEngine checks target availability before walking; rejects
  immediately if target is interacting or already talking, logs talk_rejected event
- Request Phase: initiator walks to target; pendingTalkTarget held until arrival
- Arrival re-check: Engine._handleTalkArrival re-validates target state on arrival;
  gracefully rejects if target became busy during the walk
- Synchronisation: _startConversation puts both characters into 'talking' state,
  both face each other, dialogue speech bubbles fire at conversation start
- Duration: 10–15 s random (via _talkDurationMs getter); both characters exit
  independently when their timer expires
- AI timer pause: AIEngine.update skips decisions while action === 'talking'
- Debug page: talking badge, conversation partner in Target row, hold timer,
  TALK/REJECT log entries with event consumption from pendingEvents

Co-authored-by: devlab-agent <devlab-agent@noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(data): add GetDocumentMetadata to avoid loading document BLOBs (#668)

## Summary

- Add `GetDocumentMetadata` store method that loads a document by ID
without the `Data` BLOB, `ExtractData`, or `ExtractedText` columns
- Switch `startEditDocumentForm`, `inlineEditDocument`,
`commitUpdateDocument`, and `afterDocumentSave` to use metadata-only
queries where the blob isn't needed
- `afterDocumentSave` now loads metadata first for the extraction
decision, only fetching the full blob when extraction actually proceeds
- Surface errors via `setStatusError` in three `afterDocumentSave` paths
that previously returned nil silently
- Add `BenchmarkGetDocumentByID` comparing full vs metadata loads across
blob sizes (1 KiB to 50 MiB)

Benchmark results (6 rounds, benchstat):

| Blob size | Full | Metadata | Speedup | Memory reduction |
|-----------|------|----------|---------|------------------|
| 1 KiB | 24 us | 24 us | 1x | 7% |
| 1 MiB | 1194 us | 37 us | 32x | 99.6% |
| 10 MiB | 10.8 ms | 2.6 ms | 4x | 99.96% |
| 50 MiB | 56.5 ms | 15.6 ms | 3.6x | 99.99% |

closes #468

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
